stem: A web performance study measures the response time (ms) for a sample of 15 website users. The sample mean is 245.0 ms with a sample standard deviation of 70.5 ms. However, 2 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 13 observations have a mean of 226.4 ms and standard deviation of 44.7 ms. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 245.0, 95% CI = [206.0, 284.0]. Without outliers: mean = 226.4, 95% CI = [201.6, 251.2]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [201.6, 284.0]. Point estimate: 235.7 ms.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [201.6, 284.0].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
